title: The Software Crisis: The Engine of Invention
overview: The recurring historical realization that our ambitions for software complexity have outpaced our ability to manage it with current tools.
historical_context: Formally named at the 1968 NATO Software Engineering Conference, but a recurring theme throughout computing history.
mental_model: A wall that programmers eventually hit, forcing them to invent a new paradigm (e.g., Structured, Object-Oriented, or Memory-Safe) to climb over it.
tradeoffs: Each solution to a crisis introduces its own set of trade-offs and complexities, eventually leading to the next crisis.
legacy: The primary narrative thread used in the Atlas to explain *why* languages were invented.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Trace the Software Crisis from 1968 to the present day.
Analyze how Rust reacts to the "Memory Safety Crisis".
